summary: Cross-validated Phase 003 LLM enrichment: deterministic impact analysis should remain the default; cli-opencode/opencode-go and direct DeepSeek are remote SaaS paths suitable only for explicit opt-in, while the MCP server has local ML primitives but no reusable narrative LLM client today.

title: cli-opencode must not be the implicit default for enrichWithLLM
recommendation: Amend Phase 003 so enrichWithLLM returns skipped unless an explicit provider is configured; deterministic output remains complete.

title: opencode-go is a SaaS gateway, not local-first
recommendation: Allow opencode-go only as explicit remote enrichment mode with auth preflight, timeout, redaction, and budget limits.

title: Direct deepseek/* avoids the gateway but not SaaS
recommendation: Treat direct DeepSeek as explicit hosted-provider opt-in; do not present it as local-first.

title: MCP server has local ML primitives, not a reusable narrative LLM client
recommendation: Define a new LlmNarrativeProvider interface; implement none/skipped now and defer local narrative generation unless explicitly scoped.

title: enrichWithLLM needs provider/auth/budget contract, not a boolean only
recommendation: Replace boolean-only behavior with {enabled, provider, model, timeoutMs, maxCallsPerSession, maxInputBytes, cacheKey}; default budget should be zero or one unless configured.

title: Remote LLM calls differ from XCE SaaS endpoint adoption but still leak dependency if automatic
recommendation: Permit remote narrative only as explicit, auditable augmentation; never require it for core impact-analysis output.

title: Subprocess enrichment inherits 097 and iter-005 reliability requirements
recommendation: If cli-opencode remains an option, implement a hardened helper with closed stdin, provider preflight, timeout, process cleanup, structured skipped/failed results, and prompt redaction.
